title: Fix: Parser - Tolerate Varied Command Formats` (44
original_text:
I made the parser much more tolerant, which should fix that Gemini failure.

  It now accepts:

  - fenced or unfenced git commit commands
  - single quotes: -m '...'
  - smart quotes: -m “...”
  - multiline shell continuations with \
  - extra prose before the command
  - escaped shell characters like \$

  I also changed the error message so if Gemini still returns something unusable, the popup includes
  a short raw response excerpt. That will show what Gemini actually sent back.

  Changed:

  - smart_commit_ai/commit_message.py:160
  - smart_commit_ai/gemini_client.py:148
  - tests/test_smart_commit.py:80

  Verification passed:

  - 14 tests OK
  - compileall passed

  Please try Gemini again. If it still fails, send me the new popup’s “Raw response excerpt”; that
  will be enough to handle the exact remaining format.
expected_command: git commit -m "fix(parser): tolerate varied command formats\` (44"
